name: prison_education_llm_cheating_detection_false_pos
premise: Warden Alex Tran must decide whether to keep an LLM-based writing monitor that flags incarcerated students for plagiarism in GED essays. Education director Uma Kim shows false positives cluster among English learners whose idioms match web corpora. Disabling the tool restores teacher trust but revives gang-related ghostwriter rings staff barely contained. Keeping it may revoke credits for innocent students nearing parole hearings.

agents.name: Alex Tran
agents.identity: I am the prison warden balancing rehabilitation outcomes, security intelligence from writing assignments, and fairness in disciplinary processes.
agents.goal: I want academic integrity without stripping educational hope from students whose language patterns trigger false flags.

agents.name: Uma Kim
agents.identity: I am the education director who trains teachers on culturally responsive assessment and reviews flagged essays before sanctions.
agents.goal: I want the monitor paused until ESL bias is corrected, even if that means more manual grading overtime.

choices: I will keep the monitor active and require human review for all flagged ESL students, accepting backlog delays that may miss some ghostwriter cases.
I will disable the monitor for ninety days and shift to in-person proctored writing blocks, accepting higher gang coordination windows during unsupervised drafts.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The parole board report deadline is Friday and twelve flagged cases sit unresolved. Alex Tran must choose between keeping the LLM monitor or disabling it for ninety days. What does Alex Tran do?
